hero.title: Building Lasting Peace and
hero.subtitle: The Christian Initiative for Peace, Reconciliation and Development (ICPRD) works to empower local communities and foster regional harmony through dialogue and sustainable development.

pillars.subtitle: Areas of Intervention
pillars.title: Acting for Change Through Our Three Key Areas
pillars.description: Our holistic approach ensures that peace is not just the absence of conflict, but the presence of justice and opportunities.

pillar1.title: Peacebuilding
pillar1.desc: A major player in Disarmament, Demobilization and Reintegration (DDR) in South Kivu. We facilitate the repatriation of armed groups and the socio-professional reintegration of combatants.

pillar2.title: Humanitarian Emergency
pillar2.desc: We intervene in the fields of health and nutrition whenever the need arises during our activities, with the support of our partners.

pillar3.title: Sustainable Development
pillar3.desc: We strengthen community resilience through vocational training, empowerment, and advocacy for human rights and local development.

mission.title: Our Mission: Healing through
mission.desc: The Christian Initiative for Peace, Reconciliation and Development (ICPRD) was born from the urgent need to break systemic cycles of conflict in Eastern DRC, to assist victim populations.

point1.title: Field Action and Mediation
point1.desc: Our approach is based on direct engagement, raising awareness among conflict parties, and mediation to facilitate voluntary disarmament and repatriation.

point2.title: Community Development
point2.desc: We believe lasting peace comes through development. We build training centers and support community autonomy for a stable future.

environment.title: Environmental Rehabilitation
environment.subtitle: ICPRD intervenes in environmental rehabilitation as well as advocacy to bring environmental destroyers to rehabilitate it.
environment.slogan: "The ICPRD team during the identification of mining sites exploited, destroyed, and abandoned by Chinese companies in Kitutu"

action1.title: Identification of Destroyed Sites
action1.desc: Identification of mining sites exploited, destroyed, and abandoned by companies in Kitutu, Mwenga territory, South Kivu.

action2.title: Radio Awareness
action2.desc: Awareness broadcasts on the proper exploitation of natural resources for the development of Mwenga territory at the GL Tv radio studio.

action3.title: Counseling for Victims
action3.desc: Counseling for victims of mining exploitation in Kitutu within the Banampute Grouping.

achievements.title: Our Achievements
achievements.subtitle: An overview of our lasting impact across South Kivu and North Kivu over the years.

achievement_hero.description: Discover the tangible impact of our field interventions. Each completed project represents a concrete step towards sustainable development and resilience in the DRC.

team.title: Our Leadership Team
team.subtitle: Guided by committed experts, local activists, and dedicated peacebuilders.

cta.title: Support Peace Today
cta.desc: Your contribution directly funds our community mediation programs, voluntary disarmament, and development projects in conflict-prone areas.

blog.title: Latest Reports & Testimonials
blog.subtitle: Detailed analyses and stories from our field operations across the DRC.
